commit.message:
feat(sandbox): ship playable voxel vertical slice

Summary:
- replace the static harness scene with a first-person voxel sandbox
  featuring pointer lock, movement, jumping, gravity, collisions,
  targeting, mining, and block placement
- add a deterministic mutable world model with local persistence and
  reset behavior for browser sessions
- expand gameplay tests to cover deterministic world generation,
  persistence diffs, and player collision/jump helpers

Rationale:
- HEL-100 needs one publishable route that proves the full local voxel
  loop instead of a presentation-only harness
- keeping world state deterministic and testable reduces risk for later
  deploy and gameplay follow-up work

Tests:
- npm run check
- browser smoke via Playwright script against http://127.0.0.1:4173/
  (movement, block remove/place, persistence, screenshots)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
扩展 GitHub 与 GitLab skill 为平台级 CLI 交互 (#34)

## 背景
- 现有 GitHub / GitLab skill 的名称和描述过于聚焦 issue、PR、MR，容易把触发范围限制死。
- 实际使用中还会涉及更广泛的平台交互场景，例如 GitLab wiki。
- 同时不希望在 AGENTS.md 中直接耦合具体 skill 名称，应更多依赖 skill 自身的 name 和 description
做判断。
- 另外，当前模型在执行 `git add`、`git commit`、`git push` 时容易倾向并行调用，实际会因为
`.git/index.lock` 导致部分 Git 命令失败，需要在 `git-commit` skill 中补充明确约束。

## 变更
- 将 `github-pr-issue` 重命名为 `github-cli`，将 `gitlab-mr-issue` 重命名为
`gitlab-cli`。
- 调整两个 skill 的 description 和正文说明，从 issue / PR / MR 场景扩展到平台级 CLI 交互场景。
- 在 `gitlab-cli` 中补充 wiki 场景，明确优先检查 `glab` 子命令，不足时使用 `glab api`。
- 清理 `AGENTS.md` 中对具体 skill 的直接引用，仅保留平台交互应优先使用官方 CLI 或 API、且尽量非交互的原则。
- 更新 README 中的 skill 列表说明，和新的 skill 名称与定位保持一致。
- 在 `git-commit` 中新增 Git 写操作默认串行执行的约束，明确避免并行触发多个 `git add`、`git
commit`、`git push`，并补充 `index.lock` 相关处理说明与顺序执行示例。

## 验证
- 未运行自动化测试；本次仅涉及文档与 skill 定义调整。

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix YouTube scrobbling: proper covers, artist parsing, multi-tab, restart state

YouTube improvements:
- Fetch Last.fm album art instead of video thumbnails for YouTube music
- Also fetches album name from track.getInfo for proper scrobble metadata
- Falls back to YouTube thumbnail only when Last.fm has no art
- Parse "Track - Artist" reversed titles by validating against Last.fm artist pages
- UGC videos parse artist from title, not channel name
- Strip (Official Video), [MV], (Audio) etc. from titles
- Multi-tab detection: scans ALL music tabs, picks newest changed one

Album detail:
- Filter out tiny/placeholder artwork URLs (34s, 64s, default star)
- Save artwork button (current cover → NSSavePanel)
- Save All button (all covers → folder picker, numbered files)

Scrobble timing:
- On app restart, seed accumulated time from track's elapsed position
- Progress bar and scrobble ring start at correct position, not zero
- Auto-scrobble if already past threshold on restart

API tracking wired into all 6 services via URLSession.trackedData()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[Android] Add new BpkSearchInputControl component (#2626)

Add BpkSearchInputControl component with docking support

Introduce BpkSearchInputControl — a non-editable search field with
prefix support (None, Text, Icon), focus state, clear action, and
docking (Float/Top/Middle/Bottom) for stacking controls with shared
corner rounding. Available in OnDefault and OnContrast styles.

- Extract BpkStaticFieldImpl for Select and SearchInputSummary-ReadOnly
- Extract shared field utilities into BpkFieldImplShared
- Focused border: 2dp blue with white gap; no border when unfocused
- Revert BpkSearchInputSummary to original API (no Docking)
- Add stories, snapshot tests, and README

commit.message:
Add tabbed settings, radar chart, API call tracker, live metrics

Settings redesign:
- Three tabs: General, Integrations, Developer
- General: account, scrobble behavior, appearance, quit
- Integrations: app connectors with icons/bundle IDs, API keys, supported services list
- Developer: full diagnostic dashboard

Developer tab:
- Radar chart for scoring weights (5-axis with threshold ring)
- API call tracker across all 6 services — total/error/success counts
- Per-service bar chart sorted by call volume
- Timing table: avg/min/max ms per service
- Response time sparklines for top 3 services
- Source trust horizontal bar chart
- Memory/CPU circular gauges + sparklines (from MetricsService)
- Full diagnostics: MediaRemote, virtual memory, CPU time, threads
- Cache management with clear buttons

API tracking:
- APITracker.shared records every outgoing API call
- URLSession.trackedData() extension auto-tracks all services
- Wired into Last.fm, Discogs, MusicBrainz, iTunes, Wikidata, YouTube Music

Also fixes:
- YouTube duration from API (lengthSeconds) — proper scrobble timing
- Scrobble threshold: 50% of known duration, 60s for unknown, min 30s floor
- History refreshes immediately after successful scrobble
- Menu bar reads from MediaRemote bridge for instant track updates
- Progress bar updates every second from accumulated play time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
perf(core): reduce repeated permission and replay work (#50)

Summary:
- cache permission decisions by component type during filtering and
  reuse the allowed-id set across root and subtree pruning
- compute update replay fingerprints once per normalized update
  instead of re-hashing on duplicate checks and apply
- simplify binding conflict detection to direct set intersections and
  add a regression test for repeated permission checks

Rationale:
- cut repeated authorizer and permission-policy work in a render-time
  hot path without changing the filtering contract
- remove redundant hashing from stream replay bookkeeping while
  preserving idempotency and conflict detection semantics
- keep the cleanup targeted to clarity and hot-path efficiency with
  coverage for the new permission memoization behavior

Tests:
- mix test test/json_liveview_render/permissions_test.exs test/json_liveview_render/bindings_test.exs test/json_liveview_render/stream_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add scrobble cache with retry and persistence

ScrobbleCache actor:
- Queues failed scrobbles to ~/Library/Application Support/ScrobbleNow/
- Retries pending scrobbles every 5 minutes (up to 10 attempts per entry)
- Logs successful/failed scrobble history (last 1000 entries)
- Caps pending queue at 500 entries
- Stats: pendingCount, successCount, failedCount
- Clear methods for pending and history

Integration:
- Successful scrobbles logged to history
- Failed scrobbles queued for retry automatically
- Retry timer runs every 5 minutes in background
- Total scrobbled count updates after successful retries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add expanded Now Playing, smart filtering, settings overhaul, artwork fetching

Expanded Now Playing (iOS Music-style):
- Tap compact card to expand into full-screen artwork view
- 240px album art with shadow, large title/artist/album
- Full progress bar, source app badge, scrobble progress ring
- Action buttons: Love, Discover, Edit
- Multi-source list below when multiple apps playing
- Smooth spring animation expand/collapse

Smart Filtering:
- YouTube Music API check (musicVideoType) for music vs non-music
- YouTube page category fallback (regex "category":"Music")
- All MUSIC_VIDEO_ types now return artist/track (not just OMV)
- Non-music YouTube videos silently skipped

Artwork Fetching:
- YouTube thumbnails (img.youtube.com/vi/{id}/hqdefault.jpg)
- Last.fm album.getInfo artwork
- Last.fm track.getInfo fallback
- Last.fm artist.getInfo as final fallback

Settings Overhaul:
- Scrobble Behavior section: threshold slider, min duration, podcast filter,
  album guessing, notifications toggle, live scrobble stats
- Sources section: per-app connector toggles with app icons, auto-discovered

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add native macOS scrobbling via MediaRemote + browser detection

System-wide Now Playing detection:
- MediaRemote framework bridge for Spotify, Apple Music, and native apps
- DistributedNotificationCenter for instant Spotify/Music track changes
- osascript subprocess for Chrome/Safari tab scanning (YouTube, SoundCloud,
  Bandcamp, Tidal, Deezer, Spotify Web)
- Multi-source support — shows ALL playing sources simultaneously
- 1s polling for MediaRemote, 3s for browsers with 2s timeout

Scrobble engine (SystemScrobbleService):
- Track change detection with elapsed time tracking
- Scrobble rules: >50% duration OR >4min (configurable)
- Per-app connector system with auto-discovery
- updateNowPlaying + scrobble to Last.fm

UI:
- System Now Playing card with source app icon, scrobble progress ring,
  playback progress bar, and green checkmark on scrobble
- Multiple source cards shown simultaneously
- NSAppleEventsUsageDescription for Chrome automation permission

TODO: Smart filtering (music vs non-music), scrobble cache, expanded view

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: expose raw unrolled proof access (#34)

## Summary
- re-export `UnrolledProgramProof` from `airbender-host::raw` as a raw
API without stability guarantees
- keep `RealProof::into_inner()` so callers can extract the wrapped
proof when they need direct access
- document that direct use of the raw proof is not recommended and is
intended only for rare integration cases

## Testing
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo check -p airbender-host --no-default-features`
- `cargo nextest run -p airbender-host --no-default-features`

## Notes
- `cargo check -p airbender-host` and `cargo nextest run -p
airbender-host` require the CUDA toolkit in this environment because
`gpu-prover` is a default feature; they fail here before reaching crate
validation.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Igor Aleksanov <popzxc@yandex.ru>

- keep `cycle-markers` in CI as an explicit step and make its guest
build use `--locked`
- add explicit CI steps for `fibonacci`, `std-btreemap`, and `u256-add`
that build the guest and run the host `--prove` flow through the
examples' dev prover path

- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo install --path
crates/cargo-airbender --locked --force --debug`

cargo airbender build --project examples/cycle-markers/guest --
--locked`

cargo run --release --manifest-path
examples/cycle-markers/host/Cargo.toml --locked`

cargo airbender build --project examples/fibonacci/guest -- --locked`

--locked -- --prove`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target/examples-ci
cargo airbender build --project examples/std-btreemap/guest -- --locked`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target/examples-ci
cargo run --release --manifest-path
examples/std-btreemap/host/Cargo.toml --locked -- --prove`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target/examples-ci

- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target/examples-ci

commit.message:
Fix party expense log pagination and loading loops (#133)

* fix(pwa): stabilize party expense log pagination

Summary:
- extract party expense pagination into a dedicated library with
  imperative loaders, stable snapshots, and newest-first expense
  ordering
- replace the buggy hook implementation with a Suspense-friendly
  wrapper and preload the visible expense chunks from the party route
- add unit and Playwright coverage for chunk pagination, live updates,
  and newly created expenses returning to the top of the log

Rationale:
- the previous hook could trigger infinite update loops and was hard to
  reuse safely for preloading and Suspense integration
- moving the pagination state outside the hook makes the loading model
  predictable and gives us focused tests around chunk behavior

Tests:
- pnpm lingui:extract
- pnpm lint
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts
  --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa
  exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium
  --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(pwa): stop resuspending the expense log after mount

Summary:
- remove the local ExpenseLog Suspense boundary from the party route
- keep already loaded expenses visible while new leading chunks load in
  the background
- make the expense pagination hook non-suspending and extend the unit
  coverage for leading-chunk updates

Rationale:
- once the party route has mounted we do not want new synced chunks to
  bounce the whole screen back into a pending state
- the route loader already preloads the visible expense page, so the
  hook can safely switch to background loading for follow-up updates

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec vitest run src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa typecheck
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec eslint src/hooks/usePartyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts 'src/routes/party.$partyId.tsx'
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* style: apply automatic fixes

Signed-off-by: GitHub Actions <actions@github.com>

* test(pwa): extract document cache test helper

Summary:
- add a reusable mock document cache collection for PWA unit tests
- move party expense pagination tests onto the shared cache helper
- preserve the existing expense-specific chunk builders in the test file

Rationale:
- reuse the cache double in other tests without copying subscription and
  status logic
- keep pagination tests focused on pagination behavior instead of cache
  plumbing

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec vitest run src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(context): align context window accounting

Summary:
- separate assistant-turn context usage from aggregate billing usage
- compute context occupancy as input, cache read, cache create, and output tokens
- route /context through shared helpers and add trace plus regression tests

Rationale:
- aggregate model usage can exceed the current context window and must not overwrite the latest turn snapshot
- cache and output tokens must be counted to match the displayed context occupancy
- SDK beta windows can be larger than the model lookup default, so the resolved window must use the max value

Tests:
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=test TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=1 bun test
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=test TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=1 bun run typecheck
- make lint
- make fmt

commit.message:
fix(seo): remove [slug]/loading.tsx that hides H1 from crawlers (#830)

The loading.tsx at the [slug] level created an automatic Suspense
boundary around ALL storefront sub-pages. During SSR, Next.js rendered
StorefrontPageSkeleton as the initial HTML (no H1), with actual page
content only in RSC streaming payload scripts. Crawlers that don't
execute JavaScript (Ahrefs, some Googlebot modes) only saw the
skeleton — zero H1 tags.

The home page already has its own <Suspense> wrapper with the skeleton
fallback, so this file was redundant there and harmful for SEO on all
other pages (about, privacy, faq, contact, terms, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): honor DAO RPC ordering in wagmi config (#560)

Summary:
- normalize DAO chain metadata in a shared helper reused by the dapp
  provider
- build explicit viem transports from DAO-configured RPC URLs, keep
  their fallback order stable, and fail fast when none are configured
- avoid adding the built-in mainnet chain when the DAO itself already
  runs on chain id 1

Rationale:
- duplicate mainnet entries let RainbowKit and wagmi prefer built-in
  Ethereum RPC defaults over daoConfig.chain.rpcs
- explicit fallback transports preserve DAO RPC priority and fail over
  to the next configured endpoint when one RPC is unavailable
- empty RPC lists should fail with a clear DAO-chain error instead of
  deferring to an unusable transport with no URL

Tests:
- pnpm exec eslint src/config/wagmi.ts src/providers/dapp.provider.tsx
  src/components/members-table/hooks/useMembersData.ts
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- pnpm dlx tsx -e ordered fallback transport probe
- pnpm dlx tsx -e empty RPC error probe
- curl -fsSL https://api.degov.ai/dao/config/fluence-dao?format=yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(readme): update codex alias config

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing (#827)

* fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing collision

The `.md` directory routes (e.g., `index.html.md/route.ts`) inside the
storefront `[slug]/` route tree caused a Next.js App Router collision
with the dynamic `[category]` segment. On custom domains like
ogabassey.com, the homepage resolved to a category page titled
"Index.Html.Md" instead of the merchant storefront.

Fix: Consolidate all 8 per-page `.md` route handlers into a single
catch-all API route at `/api/llm/[...segments]/route.ts`. The proxy
now intercepts `.md` requests in three contexts (custom domains,
subdomains, and slug-based paths) and rewrites them to the API handler.

The external URLs remain spec-compliant (e.g., /index.html.md still
returns markdown), but the routes no longer live in the storefront
tree where they can collide with dynamic segments.

Introduced in: c9cb732d6 (PR #785)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add colocated tests for consolidated /api/llm route (#827)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings in PR #827

- Convert NOT_FOUND constant to notFound() factory to avoid Response body
  reuse (a Response body can only be consumed once)
- Use host header for origin derivation so custom domains resolve correctly
  on Vercel instead of returning internal deployment hostname

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore multicall batching for DAO RPC reads (#559)

* fix(web): restore multicall batching for RPC reads

Summary:
- fix the shared multicall batch size to use viem's byte-based default
- configure wagmi transports from each chain's DAO RPC list via fallback
- make shared wagmi multicall batching explicit for public read traffic

Rationale:
- the previous batch size of 10 bytes split existing multicall reads into
  near-single-call requests
- RainbowKit's default transport setup only created one http transport per
  chain, which skipped the DAO RPC fallback list from config
- reducing fan-out at the shared client layer lowers pressure on public RPCs
  without rewriting each page-level hook

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
(cherry picked from commit cb724fb8759aa7ef9421e4e117be0313dfc6ee8c)

* fix(web): harden wagmi transport selection

Summary:
- preserve DAO RPC ordering by disabling viem fallback ranking
- dedupe configured chains and transports by chain id before creating wagmi
  config
- fail fast when a chain has no configured RPC URLs

Rationale:
- automated review surfaced edge cases that could silently reorder RPC
  selection or duplicate mainnet entries
- raising an explicit config error is easier to diagnose than a later
  request-time transport failure

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(tracker): close BIG-PAR-238 and BIG-PAR-239

Summary:
- mark `BIG-PAR-238` and `BIG-PAR-239` as done in the repo-native
  tracker and refill queue metadata
- sync `recent_batches` so `BIG-PAR-240` becomes the only active
  follow-up slice after the completed refill/tooling work

Rationale:
- the repo-native queue should reflect the landed refill automation work
  instead of leaving completed slices in progress on `main`
- keeping `BIG-PAR-240` active preserves momentum for the next
  documentation/recovery follow-up without draining the queue again

Tests:
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl local-issues set-state --repo .
  --local-issues local-issues.json --issue BIG-PAR-238 --state Done
  --json
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl local-issues set-state --repo .
  --local-issues local-issues.json --issue BIG-PAR-239 --state Done
  --json
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill --apply --repo . --local-issues
  local-issues.json --sync-queue-status
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill --repo . --local-issues
  local-issues.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(refill): seed queue entries and sync recent batches

Summary:
- add a `bigclawctl refill seed` subcommand that upserts queue records,
  seeds matching local tracker issues, and updates recent-batch metadata
- extend `--sync-queue-status` so it also refreshes
  `recent_batches.active/completed/standby` from the local tracker
- backfill `BIG-PAR-235` through `BIG-PAR-237` in the queue/tracker and seed
  `BIG-PAR-238` through `BIG-PAR-240` for the next repo-native batch

Rationale:
- drained refill queues should be recoverable from the CLI instead of manual
  JSON edits
- queue metadata should stay aligned with the repo-native tracker so active and
  standby batch views do not drift during local-only execution

Tests:
- go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill --help
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill seed --help
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill --repo . --local-issues local-issues.json
- bash scripts/ops/bigclawctl refill --apply --repo . --local-issues
  local-issues.json --sync-queue-status

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(queue): use nanoid for adaptive task IDs

Switch task ID generation to a custom lowercase Crockford-style Base32 alphabet via nanoid.

Keep assignment logic simple while adapting generated ID length as occupancy reaches the configured threshold and retrying on collisions before persisting new IDs.

Agent: pi

commit.message:
Fix duplicate nested item indexing

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: suppress stale signals from triggering heartbeat delivery (#32)

* fix: suppress stale signals from triggering heartbeat delivery

Two fixes for the heartbeat decision pipeline:

1. Filter expired goal_progress: Plans with days_left < 0 and status
   "stalled" or "at_risk" are now excluded from active signals. Only
   expired plans with status "on_track" (active work despite deadline)
   survive the filter.

2. Suppress low-tier-only signals without confluence: When the highest
   signal tier is "low" (patterns, sentiment, relationships, decaying)
   and the combined weight doesn't meet the confluence threshold, the
   engine now returns should_act=false instead of falling through to act.

Also gitignore demo-snapshots.json and *.db.hnsw test artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(heartbeat): preserve no-expiry goal progress

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
